brief_justification: Agent correctly identifies 3000 req/min as the effective system-wide limit with explicit derivation (3 × 1000); Goes three levels deep — root cause, per-instance math, systemic fix options; Explicitly injects reasoning context tags at the top but does not name assumptions or biases mid-analysis; Provides concrete numerical example (2400 → 800/instance) and a summary table as verification; Acknowledges the even-distribution assumption implicitly ('as long as the load balancer distributes evenly') but does not enumerate confidence levels or what would change the conclusion; Offers three distinct fix alternatives (shared counter, quota division, gossip) with tradeoffs noted; Every figure traces to an explicit derivation — 3×1000=3000, 1000/3≈333, 2400/3=800 — fully reproducible; Injection's negative gate concerns microservice restart bursts, unrelated to rate-limiting — agent avoids any scheduling anti-pattern but does not address the mismatch; Suppressed modes (synchronous_burst_tolerance, batch_clustering_bias) are domain-mismatched to the question; agent exhibits neither but cannot explicitly address them; Topology describes a temporal scheduling graph with branch/gate nodes — agent's reasoning is linear for this rate-limit problem and does not follow the branch/gate/redistribution structure.
